Patch Set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 2:

"ll_file_io_generic() is our replacement for kernel's
__generic_file_write_iter() which calls file_update_time() before
any changes. thus, we need to call it as well.
also, osc_req_attr_set() shouldn't reset mtime from old lvb.
"
This isn't right - we call __generic_file_write_iter from vvp_io_write_start() (and tiny write)

Patch Set 2:

one very strange observation is that in this sequence:
local d1=$(date +%s)
echo hello >> $DIR1/$tfile

current_time(inode) called from file_update_time() can return seconds which is less than seconds returned by date +%s

Patch Set 2:

> one very strange observation is that in this sequence:
 > local d1=$(date +%s)
 > echo hello >> $DIR1/$tfile
 > 
 > current_time(inode) called from file_update_time() can return
 > seconds which is less than seconds returned by date +%s

It could be the difference between the NTP-adjusted clock and the raw clock?  If this is a problem, we could implement our own file_update_time() method to select the clock source we need for consistency.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3:

Looks good to me.

parents.subject: LU-18501 tests: sanity.sh test_56oe fix time range

commit.subject: LU-18580 osc: don't use old mtime from lvb
commit.message:
LU-18580 osc: don't use old mtime from lvb

do not use lvb's mtime if it's older than the current one.

Test-Parameters: env=ONLY=39,ONLY_REPEAT=100 testlist=sanityn
Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I1da25d7710313d055985a39989326b9b6a52b68c
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57521
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Mikhail Pershin <mpershin@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
